description: In secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs (around line 155) and secretx-desktop/src/lib.rs (around line 143), put() does: let s = std::str::from_utf8(value.as_bytes())?.to_owned(); This creates a plain String (not Zeroizing<String>) holding the full secret value. The String is then passed to set_password(&s). If the closure exits early (panic, error) or the String is implicitly copied by the compiler, the secret bytes reside in a non-zeroizing heap allocation. Fix: use let s = zeroize::Zeroizing::new(std::str::from_utf8(value.as_bytes())?.to_owned()); and pass set_password(s.as_str()).
title: keyring and desktop put() copy secret into plain String before set_password
